title: alcea kurdica var. schirazana
intro: Alcea kurdica var. schirazana, also known as the Persian hollyhock, is a plant species in the mallow family that is native to the Middle East and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Alcea kurdica var. schirazana include its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, respiratory infections, and digestive problems. Alcea kurdica var. schirazana is typically used in herbal preparations in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and its leaves, flowers, and roots are used for medicinal purposes.
